content: Most existing discussions frame Self-Refine as a lightweight standalone framework for improving LLM outputs, while LLM self-reflection mechanisms are treated as general architectural capabilities—combining them reveals that Self-Refine can act as a modular scaffolding to ground unintegrated multiple-perspective reflection methods like Self-Contrast and Mirror, turning abstract reflective capabilities into structured, zero-training iterative improvement workflows. This creates a new hybrid paradigm that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra supervised data or training, while addressing its common limitation of relying on a single unidimensional self-feedback perspective.
